caption: Fig. 1: Decoupling view of a network into SK representing the values of the activations (left), represented by activation masks (gating); and QK, represented by the affine parameters (edge coefficients), with its activation patterns frozen (right). In FEDSQ, models are initialized from a common pretrained checkpoint and then adapted federatively by updating only QK after structural stabilization.
description2: The figure displays a decoupling view of a network, featuring SK representing activation values through masks on the left and QK represented by affine parameters with frozen activation patterns on the right.
description3: Two-panel diagram displays a network view of SK, showing activations and masks, alongside QK, displaying affine parameters with frozen activation patterns.
questions: How do activation masks and affine parameters contribute to network representation?
What is the overall purpose of decoupling activations and parameters?

caption: Fig. 2: Experiments performed over different model architec- tures and datasets, showing an improvement of FEDSQ over the other approaches. Left column indicates the experiments performed over ALEXNET. Right column indicates the exper- iments performed over RESNET18
description2: A line chart comparing the performance of various model architectures (ALEXNET and RESNET18) across different datasets, with FEDSQ showing an improvement over other approaches.
description3: Two-panel line chart displays validation accuracy versus round for FEDSQ and other approaches across AlexNet and ResNet18 architectures and datasets.
questions: What main idea or comparison does the figure present?
What overall structure or pattern is shown in the figure?
